title: DPIA Methodology (DEICA)
description: 5-step Data Protection Impact Assessment methodology aligned with the JPDP DPIA Guideline issued 30 April 2026 (originally consulted as PCP No. 1/2025).

guidelineStatus.issuingAuthority: Personal Data Protection Commissioner Shariffah Rashidah Syed Othman
guidelineStatus.verificationNote: Methodology and thresholds reflect the March 2025 consultation paper. Substantive verification against the issued PDF (https://www.pdp.gov.my/ppdpv1/akta/garis-panduan-ms/) pending. Update if issued text materially diverges from PCP.

legalBasis.description: Mandated by Section 12A of Act 709 (Amendment Act A1727) which requires Data Controllers to appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO) whose responsibilities include providing support and advice on the implementation of DPIAs.

phases.name: Describe
steps: Describe the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing
Map the personal data flow (collection, storage, use, disclosure and disposal)
Identify the categories of personal data (general vs sensitive/biometric under s4)
Identify all data processors and third parties involved
Identify any cross-border data transfers (s129)

phases.name: Evaluate
phases.description: Evaluate the necessity and proportionality (DEICA - Step 2)
steps: Assess if the processing is necessary for the stated purpose
Confirm the processing is proportionate and not excessive (s6)
Evaluate if the purpose can be achieved with less personal data
Verify the lawful basis for processing (consent vs legitimate interest s6(2))
Review data retention periods against the Retention Principle (s10)

phases.name: Identify
phases.description: Identify and analyze the specific risks (DEICA - Step 3)
steps: Identify risks of unauthorized access or disclosure (s9)
Identify risks to data subject rights (access, correction, portability s30-s43A)
Identify risks of identity theft, fraud, or financial loss
Assess risks of physical harm or serious loss of confidentiality
Consider risks to vulnerable subjects (children, employees, patients)

phases.name: Consider
phases.description: Consider measures to address the identified risks (DEICA - Step 4)
steps: Identify technical measures (encryption, pseudonymization, access control)
Identify organizational measures (policies, training, DPO oversight)
Implement data minimization and retention controls
Establish data breach notification procedures (s12B)
Conduct cross-border transfer assessments where applicable

phases.name: Assess
phases.description: Assess the overall residual risk level (DEICA - Step 5)
steps: Evaluate the likelihood and severity of risks after controls (1-5 scale)
Map residual risks to the Risk Matrix (Low, Medium, High)
Determine if the residual risk is acceptable to the organization
Notify the Commissioner (JPDP) if residual risk remains HIGH (Proposed requirement)
Obtain sign-off from the Data Protection Officer (DPO)

reviewTriggers: Material change to processing purpose or scope
New categories of personal data (especially biometric/health data)
New technology or processing method introduced
Regulatory guidance or enforcement action by JPDP
At least every two (2) years (Proposed retention requirement)
